documentation:
# fall3dutil

fall3dutil is an open source project and Python package that includes a set of utilities for downloading and pre-processing the meteorological fields required by the FALL3D model

modules.name: gfs
documentation:
Download the NCEP operational Global Forecast 
System (GFS) analysis and forecast data required
by the FALL3D model
!/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: fall3dutil.configuration.parse_float2
documentation: Read a 2-list a floats

functions.name: fall3dutil.configuration.parse_int2
documentation: Read a 2-list a integers

classes.name: fall3dutil.configuration.Config

methods.name: fall3dutil.configuration.Config.printInfo
documentation: Print attributes values

methods.name: fall3dutil.configuration.Config.date
documentation: Start and end dates

methods.name: fall3dutil.configuration.Config.lat
documentation: Latitude range

methods.name: fall3dutil.configuration.Config.lon
documentation: Longitude range

methods.name: fall3dutil.configuration.Config.step
documentation: Time step in hours

methods.name: fall3dutil.configuration.Config.res
documentation: Resolution in degrees

methods.name: fall3dutil.configuration.Config.cycle
documentation: Cycle time

methods.name: fall3dutil.configuration.Config.time
documentation: Time forecast range in hours

methods.name: fall3dutil.configuration.Config.ens
documentation: Ensemble member range

methods.name: fall3dutil.configuration.Config.server
documentation: Server for connection

methods.name: fall3dutil.configuration.Config.format
documentation: Format of output file

methods.name: fall3dutil.configuration.Config.domain
documentation: Type of domain

The Config object contains the attributes 
required by the fall3dutil classes

Parameters
----------
arg : Namespace object
    A Namespace object generated using the argparse
    module with the list of required attributes as
    defined in the self.attrs variable.
    In addition, attributes can be read from an
    input configuration file using a ConfigParser 
    object if arg.file if defined

Attributes
----------
lon : [float]
    Longitudes range

lat : [float]
    Latitudes range

time : [int]
    Time range

ens : [int]
    Ensemble members range

res : float
    Resolution in deg

cycle : int
    Cycle

step : int
    Time step in hours

format : str
    Format of output file

verbose : bool
    If print addition information

server : str
    URL server

date : [datetime]
    Dates range

Attributes

    Latitudes range

res : float
    Resolution in deg

    Cycle

step : int
    Time step in hours

verbose : bool
    If print addition information

    URL server

date : [datetime]
    Start date in first element list

modules.name: era5_sfc
documentation: Download ERA5 data (single level) required by FALL3D model.
!/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: era5_sfc.main
documentation: from fall3dutil import ERA5sfc

modules.name: gefs
documentation:
Download data from the atmospheric component of
NCEP operational global ensemble modeling suite, 
Global Ensemble Forecast System (GEFS), 
required by the FALL3D model
!/usr/bin/env python3

classes.name: fall3dutil.ecmwf.ECMWF

methods.name: fall3dutil.ecmwf.ECMWF.retrieve
documentation: Request and download data using the CDS API

methods.name: fall3dutil.ecmwf.ECMWF._getParams
documentation: Define the config dictionary required by CDS

methods.name: fall3dutil.ecmwf.ECMWF._getDatabase
documentation: Define the database required by CDS

methods.name: fall3dutil.ecmwf.ECMWF._getFname
documentation: Define the output filename

Base object to request and download  
Weather Files from ECMWF using the Climate 
Data Store (CDS) Application Program Interface (API)

Parameters
----------
arg : Namespace object
    A Namespace object generated using the argparse
    module with the list of required attributes.
    In addition, attributes can be read from an
    input configuration file using a ConfigParser 
    object if arg.file if defined

methods.name: fall3dutil.ecmwf.ERA5._getParams
documentation: Define the config dictionary required by CDS

methods.name: fall3dutil.ecmwf.ERA5._backExt
documentation: Check if extended dataset is required

methods.name: fall3dutil.ecmwf.ERA5.lon
documentation: western longitudes must be given as negative numbers

Base object to request and download ERA5 
Weather Files from ECMWF using the Climate 
Data Store (CDS) Application Program Interface (API)

Parameters
----------
arg : Namespace object
    A Namespace object generated using the argparse
    module with the list of required attributes.
    In addition, attributes can be read from an
    input configuration file using a ConfigParser 
    object if arg.file if defined

classes.name: fall3dutil.ecmwf.ERA5ml

methods.name: fall3dutil.ecmwf.ERA5ml._getParams
documentation: Define the config dictionary required by CDS

methods.name: fall3dutil.ecmwf.ERA5ml._getDatabase
documentation: Define the database required by CDS

methods.name: fall3dutil.ecmwf.ERA5ml._getFname
documentation: Define the output filename

ERAml object to request and download ERA5 
reanalysis (model levels) files from ECMWF 
using the Climate Data Store (CDS) Application 
Program Interface (API).

Parameters
----------
arg : Namespace object
    A Namespace object generated using the argparse
    module with the list of required attributes.
    In addition, attributes can be read from an
    input configuration file using a ConfigParser 
    object if arg.file if defined

Attributes
----------
lon : [float]
    Longitudes range

lat : [float]
    Latitudes range

res : float
    Resolution in deg

step : int
    Time step in hours

format : str
    Format of the output file

verbose : bool
    If print addition information

date : [datetime]
    Start and End dates in a 2-element list

CARRAml object to request and download CARRA 
reanalysis (model levels) files from ECMWF 
using the Climate Data Store (CDS) Application 
Program Interface (API).

Parameters
----------
arg : Namespace object
    A Namespace object generated using the argparse
    module with the list of required attributes.
    In addition, attributes can be read from an
    input configuration file using a ConfigParser 
    object if arg.file if defined

Attributes
----------
step : int
    Time step in hours
   
format : str
    Format of the output file

verbose : bool
    If print addition information

date : [datetime]
    Start and End dates in a 2-element list

domain : str
    Carra domain (west_domain or east_domain)
